title: vauquelinia australis
intro: Vauquelinia australis, also known as the South Desert Willow, is a shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat coughs and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, colds, and flu, providing relief from congestion and sore throats. Herbal preparations of Vauquelinia australis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and promote overall health.
